Welcome

You are standing inside a piece of Trinidad's history

✦ ✦ ✦
Games We Used to Play

Mille Fleurs has stood on this corner of St Clair since 1904. The games on the yard today, the stories being told inside, the kites being made and flown, the songs being sung, all of it belongs to a Trinidad that shaped every family here.

There is no fixed order. No wrong turn. Follow what calls you and stay as long as you like.

The Heritage Quest is at the heart of the afternoon. But there is more than enough to fill four hours without it. Move at your own pace. The yard is yours.

The Main Event

The Mille Fleurs Heritage Quest

✦ ✦ ✦

Somewhere on these grounds today, the stories of Mille Fleurs are waiting. The Quest will take you through parts of this house and yard that most people pass their whole lives without ever entering, guided by fragments of the history that made this building matter.

You will be asked to do things along the way. Some of them will feel strange. Do them anyway. You will leave with a card that belongs only to you.

How to join

Find the sign-up table on the yard facing the front of the house. Groups of any size are welcome. Sign up as early in the afternoon as you can. The Quest closes in the mid-afternoon and spots are limited.

Limited groups. Sign up early.
The Optometrist Today AR Egg Hunt

They walked this land

✦ ✦ ✦

Long before Mille Fleurs was built, long before St Clair existed, long before Trinidad was an island — extraordinary creatures roamed this ground. Their bones are still here, preserved in Trinidad's asphalt deposits.

Six Easter eggs are hidden across these grounds today. Each one contains a QR code. Scan it and point your phone at the world around you. One of them will appear.

1

Find a hidden egg somewhere on the grounds

2

Open it and scan the QR code with your phone camera

3

Point your phone at the yard and watch what appears

4

Close the egg and leave it exactly where you found it for the next explorer

Can you find all six?
Found in Trinidad

Harlan's Ground Sloth

Paramylodon harlani

The size of a car. Its bones were pulled from Trinidad's asphalt deposits — the same tar that preserved them for tens of thousands of years is still beneath this island today.

<
🦬 Meet it at Mille Fleurs
Found in Trinidad

Shasta Ground Sloth

Nothrotheriops shastensis

Smaller than Harlan's but no less strange. Two different ground sloth species walked Trinidad at the same time, grazing the same savanna that once stretched from here to Venezuela.

<
🦬 Meet it at Mille Fleurs
Found in Trinidad

Teratornis

Teratornis merriami

A flying predator with a wingspan wider than you are tall. Its fossils have been found in Trinidad. Look up when you scan — this one belongs in the sky above Mille Fleurs.

<
🦅 Meet it at Mille Fleurs
From Trinidad's world

American Mastodon

Mammut americanum

Elephant-like creatures called gomphotheres walked Trinidad — confirmed from our asphalt deposits. The Mastodon was their close relative, roaming the same vast Ice Age continent that once included this island.

<
🐘 Meet it at Mille Fleurs
From Trinidad's world

Ancient Bison

Bison antiquus

During the Ice Age, Trinidad was part of a vast savanna connected to South America. Large herbivores like this one grazed that same landscape. Trinidad was not always an island — it was once the edge of a continent.

<
🐃 Meet it at Mille Fleurs
From Trinidad's world

Columbian Mammoth

Mammuthus columbi

Thirteen feet tall. Tusks longer than a car. The Columbian Mammoth roamed the Ice Age Americas — the same world that Trinidad was part of before the sea rose and made us an island approximately 11,000 years ago.

<
🦣 Meet it at Mille Fleurs
Your discoveries today
○ ○ ○ ○ ○ ○

⭐ You found all six. You are a Prehistoric Explorer. ⭐

3D models by the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County. All models animated and scientifically accurate.

Across the Grounds

What is waiting for you

✦ ✦ ✦

The yard is full this afternoon. Here is what is open to you.

🎏 Limited Spots

Kite Making with a Local Master

Sit down with a traditional kite maker and build your own from scratch. You leave with a finished kite in your hands. Spots fill quickly. Sign up at the Camboulay Room door when you arrive.

🎏 Limited Spots

Chinese Embassy Kite Workshop

Two sessions during the afternoon. Families build together. Sign up at the yard table.

🎮

The Games Yard

Giant Ludo, Connect 4, Jenga, Snakes and Ladders, Tin Pan Alley and more. Walk up and play throughout the afternoon.

🎵

Called Games

Musical Chairs, Simon Says and more throughout the afternoon. Listen out for the MC on the front yard.

🧢 Children 12 and under

Easter Bonnet Competition

Three categories: Biggest and Boldest, Most Creative, Best Local Touch. Bring your bonnet from home. Register at the yard table. Registration closes at 3:30 PM. Winners announced at 4:50 PM.

🎊

Maypole Dancing

A classic of the old-time yard. Watch the ribbons weave on the pole or join in. Running throughout the afternoon.

🥁

Bobolee Beating

One of Trinidad and Tobago's most spirited Easter traditions. Find the Bobolee on the grounds and take your turn.

🐴

Horse Riding

Ponies at the back of the property near the market entrance. Ask any staff member for directions.

🎨

Face Painting

Find the face painter near the vendors at the back of the house. First come, first served.

🍲

Food and Refreshments

Vendors at the covered market at the back of the house. Corn soup, hot cross buns, snow cone, popcorn and more.

For the Young and the Young at Heart

Stories in the Prada Room

✦ ✦ ✦

Slip through the door on the right side of the house as you face it. Find a seat. Someone will be reading.

Two storytelling sessions run during the afternoon. No ticket, no sign-up. Just find a spot and settle in.

Early afternoon

Opens from 2:30 PM. Local authors and storytellers reading work rooted in Trinidadian life, childhood and cultural memory.

Late afternoon

Opens from 5:00 PM. A quieter, more intimate close to the day.

Storytelling programme supported by the National Library and Information System Authority of Trinidad and Tobago (NALIS).

Getting Around

Find your way around

✦ ✦ ✦

Mille Fleurs is one grand house with a yard, a covered market at the back, and more rooms than you expect.

  • Heritage Quest sign-upThe table on the front yard, facing the house
  • Bonnet registrationThe table on the front yard, facing the house
  • Maypole DancingFront yard area
  • Bobolee BeatingSomewhere on the grounds. Look for the crowd.
  • Kite Making sign-upAt the Camboulay Room door, left side ground floor
  • Games YardFront yard from the main steps outward
  • StorytellingInside the house, right side as you face it. The Prada Room.
  • Local Kite MakingInside the house, left side ground floor
  • Embassy Kite WorkshopUpstairs in the Ballroom
  • Traditional GamesSalvatori Room, inside the house
  • Food and vendorsCovered market at the back of the house
  • Horse ridingBack of the property near the market entrance
  • Face paintingNear the vendors at the back
  • Picnic areaLeft side of the grounds under shade
  • Egg collection basketNear the gate on your way out
  • RestroomsAt the back of the house near the market area.

After Today

Bring your event
to Mille Fleurs

✦ ✦ ✦

You are standing in one of the most distinctive event spaces in Trinidad. The Victorian verandah. The fountain yard. The ballroom upstairs. The intimate rooms inside. Mille Fleurs is available to hire for private events, corporate functions, weddings, launches, and celebrations of all kinds.

If today has given you an idea, we would love to hear it.

Weddings and ceremonies

The verandah, the fountain, the yard. A setting that needs no decoration to be extraordinary.

Corporate and private events

Dinners, launches, receptions, gatherings. Spaces that carry history into every occasion.

Photography and film

The architecture, the light, the grounds. Available by arrangement for shoots and productions.

Cultural and community events

NTT actively partners with organisations whose work connects to heritage, culture and community life.

Tell us about your event

Thank you. We will be in touch shortly.

Become a Member of the National Trust

NTT membership connects you to the ongoing work of protecting Trinidad and Tobago's built and natural heritage. Members receive priority access to events, invitations to private site visits, and the knowledge that they are part of something that matters.

Become a Member
Who We Are

The National Trust of Trinidad and Tobago

✦ ✦ ✦

The National Trust of Trinidad and Tobago is a membership-based organisation established by Act of Parliament in 1991 to preserve and safeguard the built and natural heritage of Trinidad and Tobago. We are vested with the authority to identify, list, protect and promote the historic properties, archaeological sites and natural landscapes that form the physical legacy of our nation's past.

Mille Fleurs is one of the properties under our care. Events like today bring people into direct contact with that heritage and with the organisation that keeps it alive. We are glad you are here.

To learn more about NTT, our work, or how to get involved, speak to any member of our team today or reach us at the contact below.

Before You Leave

How was your afternoon?

✦ ✦ ✦

You were here. Tell us what you thought. It takes two minutes and it helps us make the next one even better.

Share Your Thoughts
